retrieved_docs.title: Tonometry -- Technique or Treatment -- Technique Considerations
retrieved_docs.content: Clean and disinfect all contact tonometers between patients unless they utilize disposable covers. This assists in preventing disease transmission and possible corneal abrasions from residual debris.

retrieved_docs.title: Onychoscopy -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Both nonpolarized and polarized light dermatoscopes are utilized in onychoscopy. To minimize or eliminate surface reflectance, the difference in refractive indices between air and the stratum corneum must be addressed. [32] This reduction in reflectance can be accomplished by using a polarized dermoscope, adjusting the dermoscope at a tangential angle, and applying an interface medium. Various interface media have been used during onychoscopy, such as antiseptic gels (eg, alcohol), water-based gels (eg, ultrasound gel), or oils (eg, mineral oil). Among these, ultrasound gel is preferred owing to its decreased viscosity, which allows it to stay in contact with the nail plate and fill up the concavities. [43] While performing onychoscopy, keeping the digit gently on a flat hard surface is recommended, avoiding any undue pressure.

retrieved_docs.title: Keratometer -- Interfering Factors -- Limitations
retrieved_docs.content: Corneal irregularities like abrasion, erosions, epithelial defects, etc., will preclude the perfect keratometry reading due to irregular astigmatism. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Viscoelastics -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Surface Tension
retrieved_docs.content: It is a property by which a viscoelastic coat the surface. This property is also governed by the contact angle formed by a drop of viscoelastic on a flat surface. Lower surface tension and low contact angle have better coating ability. The surface tension of sodium hyaluronate is less; hence it has a better coating ability. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Keratometer -- Interfering Factors -- Limitations
retrieved_docs.content: The peripheral corneal surface is not considered while having refractive measurements. The keratometers take into account only the central curvature of 3 to 4 mm, which is a smaller portion and is a potential limitation. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Onychoscopy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: A dermatoscope is required for onychoscopy. For certain types of onychoscopy, alcohol wipes and a contact medium (such as ultrasound gel) may be required. [40] Modern handheld dermatoscopes can switch between polarized and nonpolarized settings; if not, separate dermatoscopes for each function are available. Handheld dermatoscopes may be combined with digital cameras or smartphones to capture images or videos (see Image. Dermatoscope, Handheld and Video). Nail plate changes such as pits, scales, surface irregularities, or matrix diseases are appreciated well with noncontact onychoscopy. In contrast, nail bed changes, color abnormalities, onycholysis, and changes in the distal nail margin are better visualized by contact onychoscopy. For optimal vasculature evaluation, the hand should be at the level of the heart, and average ambient temperatures should be maintained. [41]

retrieved_docs.title: Skin Resurfacing Dermabrasion -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Rotary wire brush tips consist of 2-3 mm wires protruding from a central cylinder. They are available in a wide range of sizes, shapes, and coarseness. In contrast to diamond fraises, these induce injury by the production of microlacerations in the skin. [14] They can penetrate the skin with even mild pressure, and therefore speeds greater than 25,000 RPM are not advised. [15] [2] Manual dermabrasion with sterile sandpaper or drywall sanding screen may be employed for the treatment of more localized areas, such as scar revision. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Contact Lenses -- Technique or Treatment -- Lathe Cutting
retrieved_docs.content: This is followed by edges, the peripheral, and the intermediate curve modification. Contact lenses are then modified and verified based on these parameters. The significant advantage of lathe cutting is that in soft lenses, the lenses can be modified and engraved for identification, good quality surface finishing can be obtained nearly equal to that of PMMA lenses, the dimensional accuracy can also be maintained by lens evaluation in the dry state. [50]

retrieved_docs.title: Keratometer -- Interfering Factors -- Limitations
retrieved_docs.content: It employs para axial optics to measure the corneal surface power. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: The need and method for surface cleansing of pits and fissures before placing a sealant vary among operators. Some have suggested using pumice or air-polishing instruments to obtain an optimal acid-etch pattern of the enamel. At the same time, others consider acid etching alone as sufficient for surface cleaning. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Photorefractive Keratectomy -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Operating microscope and illumination system: A high-quality surgical microscope provides magnification and coaxial illumination, which are essential for visualization during PRK. Key features include the following: Adjustable magnification (typically ×6 to ×25) Anti-glare coating to reduce corneal reflection Coaxial LED or halogen illumination Compatibility with the laser interface Microscopes from Carl Zeiss, Leica, and Haag-Streit are widely used in ophthalmic laser suites. [60] Corneal pachymetry device: Central corneal thickness is a critical preoperative measurement for PRK candidacy and safety. Devices used include the following: Ultrasound pachymeter: Handheld, high-frequency probe for point-based thickness measurements. Optical pachymetry: Included in systems such as Pentacam or optical coherence tomography (OCT), offering pachymetric mapping across the cornea. Ensuring adequate residual stromal thickness post-ablation (typically >250 µm) is essential to avoid ectasia. [61]

retrieved_docs.title: Skin Resurfacing Dermabrasion -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Diamond fraises are industrial diamond chip-studded tips made in a variety of shapes, including cone, cylinder, wheel, bullet, and pear. The grit is variable, with finer fraises suited for dermabrasion of small areas, delicate skin, or superficial scars and coarser fraises preferred for deep scars and full-face dermabrasion. [13] [1] [8] Diamond fraises abrade the skin through frictional injury. The diamond projections are very small, minimizing the risk of penetrating too deeply in the skin as long as the tool is handled properly. [2] Diamond fraises are easier to control than wire brushes, making them more suitable for novice surgeons. [8] [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: One of the technical complications reported in the YTZ-P reconstructions is chipping or delamination of the veneering porcelain. The core-veneer interface is a critical factor in the success of layered restorations. The contributing factors for the fracture may include weakness of the veneer material or the core/veneer bond, stresses and distortion due to the veneering process, unsupported veneering porcelain and configuration of the core and veneer, residual stresses arising from a coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch, and rapid cooling rates after heat treatment. The use of high-speed polishing rotary instruments is contraindicated for clinical use because of nickel contamination, phase changes in zirconia, and the resultant surface roughness.

retrieved_docs.title: Specular Microscopy  -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Computerized analysis: The computerized analysis helps to assess the cell density, frequency, size, and shape. [3] Subjective decision-making can induce errors in the center-to-center method and cell border. The accuracy of endothelial cell morphology depends on the clarity and quality of the endothelial scan obtained. The specular scans are based on the specular reflex. Hence, any barrier in the endothelial monolayer's optical pathway will affect the scan quality. The other factors hampering scan quality include tear film abnormalities, epithelial haze, stromal scar, guttae, and DM damage. Based on the quality of the endothelial scan, it can be graded as good, fair, poor, or impossible. [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Corneal Topography -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The index of surface variance (ISV) is the deviation of the corneal radius as compared to the mean value. ISV may be useful for tracking progression.

retrieved_docs.title: Corneal Topography -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Tear film abnormalities and contact lens wear can alter corneal topography maps. In addition, the patient's eye movements, ptosis, and improper placement of the patient's head on chin rest can cause problems in data acquisition. Comparison between topography maps of the same patient cannot be made between devices. K1, K2, and sim K astigmatism may not be the same across all devices. There are several indices that are unique to a particular device. K max, for instance, is measured by Schiempflug tomography and not by Orbscan or Placido-based instruments.

Collectively, these entities illustrate an integrated framework for fracture fixation, deformity correction, growth modulation, prosthetic revision, and preventive care in thigh and leg surgery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 3 -- Community 1918
retrieved_docs.content: The radius is identified as an allowed qualifier in several specialized domains, indicating that it can be the focus of detailed study or intervention in each area. In ultrastructural research, the radius is an allowed qualifier, meaning that subcellular imaging and analysis are applicable to this bone. Anatomical and histological investigations also list the radius as an allowed qualifier, reflecting its significance in the study of bone structure and tissue composition. Cytological assessment includes the radius as an allowed qualifier, allowing cellular‑level examination of bone cells. In the context of transplantation, the radius is an allowed qualifier, signifying that it may be considered for grafting or transplant procedures. Clinically, the radius is the direct_procedure_site_of treatment for fractures or dislocations of the radius and ulna, highlighting its central role in orthopedic care and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: The category “THIGH AND LEG: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS” comprises a comprehensive set of operative interventions that target the femur and adjacent structures. Child procedures include epiphyseal arrest techniques—“Arrest, epiphyseal, any method (eg, epiphysiodesis); combined distal femur, proximal tibia and fibula,” “Arrest, epiphyseal, any method (eg, epiphysiodesis); distal femur,” and “Arrest, hemiepiphyseal, distal femur or proximal tibia or fibula (eg, genu varus or valgus)”—which modulate growth plates to correct deformities. Fracture‑stabilizing operations encompass “Femoral shortening with blade plate,” “Internal fixation of femur” (with and without fracture reduction), “Intramedullary nailing of femur,” “Open reduction of fracture of femur” (with or without internal fixation), and “Trochanterplasty.” Osteoplastic modifications are represented by “Osteoplasty of femur,” “Osteoplasty of femur, lengthening,” “Osteoplasty of femur, shortening,” and “Osteoplasty, femur; combined, lengthening and shortening with femoral segment transfer,” allowing alteration of femoral length and contour. Preventive and corrective measures include “Prophylactic treatment of femur,” “Repair of malunion, of femur, distal to head and neck,” and “Suture of periosteum of femur.” Prosthetic management is covered by “Replacement of prosthesis of femur with use of methyl methacrylate” and “Revision of total knee arthroplasty, all components (procedure).” Clinically, these procedures address the primary anatomical site of lower‑limb fractures, exemplified by the entity “fracture of lower limb” classified as an unspecified closed fracture of the femoral neck, underscoring the femur’s central role in diagnosis, fracture stabilization, growth modulation, length alteration, and implant revision within orthopedic care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 362
retrieved_docs.content: Other bone disease and musculoskeletal deformities are associated with bone resorption, indicating that these conditions often involve increased breakdown of bone tissue, which contributes to skeletal weakening and structural abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2809
retrieved_docs.content: Magnetic resonance imaging (تصوير بالرنين المغناطيسي) is classified as a specific modality within the broader category of imaging, visualization, and scanning techniques. It also functions as a key component of bioimaging and biomedical imaging, linking advanced magnetic resonance technology to the overall field of medical imaging used for diagnostic and research purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 154
retrieved_docs.content: pathology or correct malunited fractures, while “Suture of periosteum of femur” supports periosteal integrity for bone healing. Prosthetic and joint‑related interventions involve “Replacement of prosthesis of femur with use of methyl methacrylate,” “Revision of total knee arthroplasty, all components (procedure),” and “Trochanterplasty,” which address femoral implant revision, comprehensive knee arthroplasty component exchange, and greater trochanter reshaping to improve hip biomechanics. Collectively, these procedures illustrate the extensive, femur‑centered surgical repertoire for managing growth disturbances, fractures, malunions, deformities, and prosthetic complications, emphasizing the need for individualized operative planning based on anatomical site, underlying pathology, and desired functional outcome.

retrieved_docs.content: Disease condition determination, uncontrolled is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, indicating that an uncontrolled disease state is linked to the presence of diabetes mellitus that has progressed to a complicated form, underscoring the importance of effective disease management to prevent or mitigate diabetic complications.
